title: Link ad accounts to a Payment Portfolio
content:
Use this endpoint to link an ad account to a Payment Portfolio. ## Request **Endpoint** https://business-api.tiktok.com/open_api/v1.3/payment_portfolio/advertiser/update/ **Method** POST **Header** ```xtable |Field{30%}|Data Type{15%}|Description{55%}| |---|---|---| |Access-Token {Required}|string|Authorized access token. For details, see [Authentication](https://business-api.tiktok.com/portal/docs?id=1738373164380162).| |Content-Type {Required}|string|Request message type.
Allowed format: `application/json`. | ``` **Parameters** ``` xtable |Field{30%}|Data Type{15%}|Description{55%}| |--- |--- |--- | |payment_portfolio_id{Required}|string|The ID of the Advanced Payment Portfolio.

To obtain a list of available Payment Portfolio IDs, use [/payment_portfolio/get/](https://business-api.tiktok.com/portal/docs?id=1839151931301954) and filter for Payment Portfolios where the `payment_portfolio_type` is `SHARED`.| |advertiser_ids{Required}|string[]|The list of ad accounts that you want to link to the Payment Portfolio.
You can only link ad accounts that belong to the same client as the Payment Portfolio.

Max size: 20.

  • If the ad account belongs to a Business Center, you need to have Finance Manager or Admin access to the Business Center. To confirm your access to a Business Center, use [/bc/get/](https://business-api.tiktok.com/portal/docs?id=1737115687501826) and check the returned `user_role` and `finance_role`.
  • If the ad account does not belong to a Business Center, you need to have Admin or Operator access to the ad account.

Note: If `advertiser_ids` contains any invalid IDs, the whole call will fail.| ``` ### Example ```xcodeblock (code curl http) curl --location --request POST 'https://business-api.tiktok.com/open_api/v1.3/payment_portfolio/advertiser/update/' \ --header 'Access-Token: {{Access-Token}}' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "payment_portfolio_id": "{{payment_portfolio_id}}", "advertiser_ids": ["{{advertiser_id}}"] }' (/code) ``` ## Response ``` xtable |Field{30%}|Type{15%}|Description{55%}| |---|---|---| |code |number|Response code. For the complete list of response codes and descriptions, see [Appendix-Return Code](https://business-api.tiktok.com/portal/docs?id=1737172488964097). | |message|string|Response message. For details, see [Appendix - Return Codes](https://business-api.tiktok.com/portal/docs?id=1737172488964097).| |request_id |string|The log ID of a request, which uniquely identifies the request. | |data |object|Returned data.| ``` ### Example ```xcodeblock (code Success-Response http) HTTPS/1.1 200 OK { "code": 0, "message": "OK", "request_id": "{{request_id}}", "data": {} } (/code) ```
